The previous attempt had errors: it used an unknown AscendC::Dup function, and also tried to InitBuffer with a TBuf (scalarBuf_) which is not supported (needs TQue). The log_softmax operation needs to compute row-wise max, subtract, sum, log, and subtract again. On Ascend, typically we use a two-pass algorithm: first pass compute row max, second pass compute sum of exp(x - max), then final pass compute log_softmax = x - max - log(sumExp). However, in the UB (Unified Buffer) kernel we have limited local memory, so we need to do it tile by tile. But we can compute row max and row sum across the total length per row. Since the input is (batch_size, dim) with dim=393216 and batch=4096, each row is very long. We can tile along the row dimension: we process a tile of the row, update the max and sum gradually. Then after the first pass we have the global row max and row sum for that row, then we need to store those per row. We could do a two-pass kernel: pass1: per row compute max and sum and store them in global memory (a separate buffer), pass2: read back those per-row constants and compute final output.

But the kernel signature: log_softmax_custom(GM_ADDR x, GM_ADDR y, GM_ADDR workspace, GM_ADDR tiling). We can use workspace to store intermediate per-row max and sum.

The tiling data should include: totalLength, outer (batch size), inner (dim per row), tileLength, blockDim. Also we need to store negative_slope? No, log_softmax has only dim attribute. But in our case, dim is always 1 (the last dimension). We'll ignore dim attribute; we'll assume log_softmax is always over the last dimension (C). We'll treat the tensor as 2D (outer, inner). If dim=1 (last dim), then outer = batch_size, inner = dim. We'll compute log_softmax over each row.

The log_softmax operation needs to compute row-wise max, subtract, sum, log, and subtract again. On Ascend, typically we use a two-pass algorithm: first pass compute row max, second pass compute sum of exp(x - max), then final pass compute log_softmax = x - max - log(sumExp). However, in the UB (Unified Buffer) kernel we have limited local memory, so we need to do it tile by tile. But we can compute row max and row sum across the total length per row.
